SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.view.manager

Class GameInputHandler

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.view.manager.GameInputHandler
public class GameInputHandler extends Object
Responsible for handling all keyboard input and user interactions.

This class processes keyboard events and delegates them to the appropriate game controller methods. It supports both single-player and two-player game modes, with different control schemes for each player.

Control Schemes:

  • Single-Player Mode:
    • A/D: Move left/right
    • W: Rotate clockwise
    • F: Rotate counter-clockwise
    • S: Soft drop
    • Space: Hard drop
    • Shift: Hold piece
  • Two-Player Mode:
    • Player 1: Same as single-player (WASD + Space + Shift + F)
    • Player 2: Arrow keys + Numpad (←/→/↑/↓ + 0 for hard drop + 2 for rotate CCW + 3 for hold)
  • Global Controls:
    • P: Pause/Resume
    • N: New game
    • M: Mute/Unmute

Design Pattern: Extracted from GuiController to adhere to Single Responsibility Principle (SRP)

Version:
Phase 3 - SRP Refactoring
Author:
Dong, Jia

  • Constructor Details

    • GameInputHandler

      public GameInputHandler(javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty isPause, javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty isGameOver)
      Constructs a GameInputHandler.
      Parameters:
      isPause - the pause state property
      isGameOver - the game over state property

  • Method Details

    • setEventListener

      public void setEventListener(InputEventListener eventListener)
      Sets the event listener for game controller events.
      Parameters:
      eventListener - the input event listener

    • setCallbacks

      public void setCallbacks(GameInputHandler.InputHandlerCallbacks callbacks)
      Sets the callbacks for input handler actions.
      Parameters:
      callbacks - the callback interface implementation

    • setTwoPlayerMode

      public void setTwoPlayerMode(boolean isTwoPlayer)
      Sets the game mode (two-player or single-player).
      Parameters:
      isTwoPlayer - true for two-player mode, false for single-player

    • handleKeyPressEvent

      public void handleKeyPressEvent(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent keyEvent)
      Handles keyboard key press events. Routes the event to the appropriate handler based on game mode and state.
      Parameters:
      keyEvent - the KeyEvent containing information about the key press
